mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a certain threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as profit margins are squeezed and discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and industrial production is accelerating, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment supporting real economic growth, which boosts corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combination of low FEDFUNDS and rising INDPRO fails to produce positive SPX 20-day returns in a statistically significant manner (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production growth, the economy demonstrates strong productive capacity, which supports corporate earnings and justifies higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially when inflation is contained.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 3.0% while INDPRO is growing, and SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts indicates strong investment in residential construction and consumer confidence, acting as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds the threshold, SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly strong non-farm payrolls growth signals a robust labor market and healthy consumer base, boosting investor sentiment and expectations for future economic growth, which drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive reaction to PAYEMS monthly changes exceeding 200k over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, while also pressuring the central bank to tighten monetary policy, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 3.0% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and low unemployment indicates a healthy, growing economy with full employment, which provides a strong foundation for corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of periods when both GDPC1 growth is strong and UNRATE is low over a 5-year period.
